summary: Provide a guided workflow for verified App feedback
expected_behavior: A user can prepare a privacy-safe report and review the exact payload before choosing a delivery route.
actual_behavior: Users currently assemble feedback manually from separate observations and support guidance.
problem_statement: App users need a repeatable way to distinguish observed behavior from assumptions before reporting a problem or proposal.
user_scenarios: A user validates an App defect without changing Home Assistant.
A user compares a proposal with the current documented capability.
A user reviews the exact public payload before any external write.
current_workaround: Users manually assemble reports from terminal observations and the support checklist.
existing_capability: The support guide describes report content, but it does not produce a structured local bundle.
alternatives: Use an unstructured issue body, which does not preserve check boundaries.
Attach complete diagnostics, which would collect more data than the report needs.
acceptance_criteria: A bug or feature request creates one isolated report bundle.
No GitHub issue is created before the exact preview is confirmed.
Unavailable direct submission returns the manual Issue Form route.
compatibility_security_impact: Existing Home Assistant configuration remains unchanged and persistent GitHub credentials remain opt-in.
validation_plan: Validate both report kinds with local structured fixtures.
Reject privacy-sensitive input before creating public output.
Verify direct and manual delivery routes against one fixed repository.

checks.name: Current support guidance inspection
checks.evidence: The support checklist defines required report facts and redaction boundaries.

checks.name: Live external submission
checks.evidence: An external write was outside the read-only proposal review.

evidence: The existing support flow requires users to structure evidence manually.
unverified_scope: No live external submission was attempted.
